market_data_input.risks: Secteurs en difficulté: financials, consumer-discretionary, healthcare | Régions en difficulté: chile, india, south-africa | 3 secteurs avec 6M négatif (momentum moyen-terme dégradé)
market_data_input.sector_momentum_summary: financials(avoided,b=0.91,d=2.79%), consumer-discretionary(neutral,b=1.21,d=0.44%), energy(favored,b=-0.16,d=0.13%), utilities(neutral,b=0.75,d=-1.07%), materials(favored,b=0.8,d=0.03%), healthcare(neutral,b=0.52,d=2.81%), consumer-staples(neutral,b=0.24,d=0.11%), industrials(favored,b=0.95,d=1.27%), real-estate(neutral,b=0.42,d=1.46%), information-technology(favored,b=1.52,d=0.06%), communication-services(neutral,b=1.21,d=0.38%)

ai_response.regime_rationale: Brent near $100 + PCE at 3.77% (well above target) + Core MoM 0.38% (annualizing ~4.5%) + Fed on hold for 6 months signals persistent inflation with no easing relief. VIX at 22, IG spreads at 135bps, and mixed equity signals (S&P up but Nasdaq down) suggest growth uncertainty without outright crisis — classic stagflationary tension.

adjustments.action: Increase energy/oil cap — Brent near $100, energy leading YTD +31%
adjustments.rationale: Brent $98 with sustained momentum; energy is the natural stagflation hedge and RADAR's top sector.

adjustments.action: Reduce AI/semi exposure — high beta growth vulnerable to sticky inflation + no rate cuts
adjustments.rationale: Nasdaq underperforming S&P today (-0.48% vs +0.38%); PCE 3.77% kills the rate-cut narrative that supports growth multiples.

adjustments.action: Increase precious metals cap — gold and silver as inflation/uncertainty hedge
adjustments.rationale: Gold at $4469 despite 16% drawdown from ATH signals strong structural demand; silver $73 confirms real-asset bid in stagflationary environment.

adjustments.action: Increase dividend/value tilt — defensive quality outperforms in stagflation
adjustments.rationale: Utilities outperforming today (+0.53%); value/dividend stocks provide real income and lower duration risk vs growth.
Stagflation + geopolitical oil risk + dollar weakening (DTWEXBGS 118.9 < 120 neutral) = gold as core hedge.

adjustments.action: Prefer TIPS, shorten duration, avoid HY — inflation protection with credit caution
adjustments.rationale: PCE 3.77% + breakeven 2.48% = TIPS underpriced vs realized inflation; HY spreads 275bps look tight for stagflation risk — asymmetric downside.

adjustments.action: Raise bond floor for Stable — increase defensive allocation
adjustments.rationale: Stable at only 20.8% bonds with 79.2% equity is too aggressive for a stagflationary regime with VIX 22.

adjustments.action: Raise tactical cash — VIX 22 + regime uncertainty warrants dry powder
adjustments.rationale: HYG down -0.50% and LQD down -0.38% today signal credit stress building; cash provides optionality to buy a potential dip if spreads blow out.

bond_strategy.regime_summary: Stagflation playbook: TIPS for inflation protection, treasury short-end for safety, avoid HY credit risk, duration capped at 4y to limit rate sensitivity with Fed on hold and inflation sticky.

cash_allocation.rationale: VIX 22 + credit spreads widening intraday + stagflation uncertainty = maintain dry powder; T-bills at ~3.6% yield make cash productive while waiting for clarity.

crypto_allocation.rationale: Crypto is a risk asset that suffers in stagflation (no liquidity tailwind, no rate cuts); cap reduced but not eliminated for Agressif given structural BTC thesis.

warnings: OIL SUPPLY SHOCK RISK: Brent at $98 approaching $100 psychological level — any geopolitical escalation (Middle East, OPEC+ surprise) could spike to $110+ and tip stagflation into crisis
CREDIT DETERIORATION: HYG -0.50% and LQD -0.38% today despite S&P positive — equity/credit divergence is a classic early warning of risk-off transition
FED POLICY ERROR: PCE 3.77% vs Fed Funds 3.62% means REAL rates are barely positive — if inflation reaccelerates, forced hikes into slowing growth = recession risk
CURVE STEEPENING TRAP: 2s10s at +41bps after likely inversion = bear steepening (long rates rising on inflation fears), not bull steepening — this is NOT a green light
STABLE PORTFOLIO MISALIGNMENT: 79.2% equity in 'Stable' profile is dangerously high for any non-goldilocks regime — needs urgent rebalancing toward bonds/cash

market_interpretation.geopolitical_risk: Brent near $100 implies elevated geopolitical/supply risk (likely Middle East or OPEC+ driven); energy sector negative beta (-0.16) suggests oil is trading on its own supply dynamics, not correlated with broader equity risk.

adjustments_output.cash_tactical_rationale: VIX 22 + credit spreads widening intraday + stagflation uncertainty = maintain dry powder; T-bills at ~3.6% yield make cash productive while waiting for clarity.
